assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a foreign currency from the perspective of at least one group entity.
The question concerns accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the non-hedge accounting model for translating foreign currency monetary items and recognising resulting exchange differences.
